betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Linker§f (libvulpes:linker) is a §6single item tool§f used to bind two AR machines or tiles together for §6data transmission§f, §6power sharing§f, or §6network communication§f. Without a Linker, you cannot connect a §6Guidance Computer§f to a §6Rocket Assembling Machine§f, bind a §6Beacon§f to a §6Docking Pad§f, or pair a §6Wireless Transceiver§f to another transceiver.

§6Production Path (libvulpes linker recipe)§f:
- The Linker is crafted from a §6vanilla crafting table§f (no machine required)
- Ingredients: 1x §6Redstone§f, 1x §6Gold Ingot§f, 1x §6Iron Ingot§f
- Output: 1x §6Linker§f (libvulpes:linker)
- The recipe is loaded from the libVulpes jar (not the AR source distribution)

The Linker is used by §6shift-right-clicking§f the first tile, then §6right-clicking§f the second tile. The two tiles are now bound — they share data and respond to each other's state changes. The Linker is §6not consumed§f on use; it can bind an unlimited number of pairs.

The Linker is a prerequisite for the §6Guidance Computer§f (10028), the §6Space Station Assembler§f (10034), the §6Wireless Transceiver§f (10085), the §6Satellite Terminal§f (10041), and the §6Warp Controller§f (10038). It is the §6most-used tool§f in the AR progression.

§eTip§f: Keep at least 4 Linkers in your hotbar at all times. The Linker is §6not consumed§f, so you can reuse the same one for many bindings. For complex networks, label your Linkers with §6anvils§f to remember which is bound to which pair.
